solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    string reverseWords(string s) {
        int n = s.length();
        int i = 0;
        while (i < n) {
            int j = i;
            while (j < n && s[j] != ' ') {
                j++;
            }
            int left = i, right = j - 1;
            while (left < right) {
                swap(s[left], s[right]);
                left++;
                right--;
            }
            i = j + 1;
        }
        return s;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The algorithm processes each word in the string by reversing its characters in-place. It iterates through the string once, identifying word boundaries and reversing characters within those boundaries. This approach is efficient as it avoids unnecessary memory allocation and operates in linear time.
